title: Mildly Spicy Ethiopian Lamb Tagine
description: A buttery and aerated Ethiopian dish featuring coriander seeds, perfect for a date night, this mildly spicy lamb tagine is cooked to perfection in a scallop shell vessel, adhering to ayurvedic tamasic friendly principles.
instructions: Heat the butter in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the coriander seeds and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the lamb and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the berbere spice, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the chicken broth and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 hour, or until the lamb is tender.
Serve the tagine in scallop shell vessels, garnished with fresh herbs, if desired.
